programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble becomes a pawn in a play for love and fortune.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is deputized then called upon to transport a man charged with a crime to the county seat.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is forced to tackle a case with a personal element when his father is discovered drunk in a car that has a dead body in the boot. The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are forced to undergo psychological evaluations conducted by Dr Rachel Cranston (guest star Wendy Makkena) while investigating the death of a submarine's commanding officer. Suspicion falls on a journalist seen arguing with the man before his demise, and Tony discovers a link between the psychotherapist shadowing the agents and Ziva's predecessor Kate Todd. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. The agents realise he may have been killed in error, and that the real target was his ex-girlfriend, a skilled player of computer games who unwittingly uncovered a mysterious code. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.desc: The team helps to facilitate secret peace talks to prevent a third world war when a conflict in Eastern Europe threatens to escalate. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A billionaire hires the team to help when his daughter is kidnapped. Meanwhile, Walter upsets the gang when he is interviewed and fails to acknowledge the other members. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. At HQ, Hetty shows the team surveillance footage of the shooting and identifies the victim as Daniel Su, an engineer who defected from North Korea and was working for the US government on a highly classified research project. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Smithy gets a warm welcome when he reports for duty at Sun Hill, but is taken aback to discover he's assigned to investigate his former cellmate Morris Leatherhead. Single dad Neil Manson has to tell James Tennant the search for his daughter is to be scaled down, and learns how painful that must feel when his own son is reported missing from school. Suzie makes her disappointment clear

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zain and Terry bend the rules over the beating of a suspected paedophile, thinking they have the backing of new boss Stuart Turner. Phil Hunter clashes with an attractive solicitor who seems set to tear his case apart, but a taste of his trademark charm weakens her resolve. Morris Leatherhead trashes a kebab shop in a bid to get back to prison. Footballers' Wives star Phina Oruche guests

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tony Stamp swallows his pride and takes on a second job as a cabbie to pay for his father's care home. Phil Hunter's response to the attack on brother Steve convinces the PC he's not wanted at Sun Hill and he applies for a transfer. Banter in the pub about Will's conquest of Honey Harman brings the relationship to a dramatic halt, while colleagues notice Sam is cosying up to new boy Stuart Turner

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Wealthy widow Alice Costigan invites Robert to stay at her country house for a spot of pheasant shooting, filling him with high hopes of romance. However, he's not the only one vying for her affections, and when her nephew falls ill she snubs him by turning to his rival for help. Victorian medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder, Maureen Beattie and Andrew Connolly

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Finlay temporarily hangs up his stethoscope to intervene in a confrontation between Irish farm labourers and irate locals who blame them for a typhus outbreak, and resolves matters with a combination of tough talking and common sense. David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Cameron feels he's being forced out of the practice now that Finlay and Neil are partners. Medical drama, starring David Rintoul and Ian Bannen

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends an exclusive auction, while Daisy and Rose try to cool the ardour of a woman who seems determined to marry Daddy. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Judy Cornwell and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth cannot suppress her excitement when she hears Emmet is rehearsing an amateur production of The Boyfriend, and resolves to land herself a part in the show. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Although Emmet's musical is already fully cast, Hyacinth remains convinced that his shyness is the only reason she has not been given a starring role. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, David Griffin and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigates the shooting of a murder suspect, and there is no shortage of potential culprits, with his angry wife and vengeful brother high on the list. There's just one little puzzle to solve before the cops can even think about whodunit - at the time of the killing, the victim was in police custody, behind three locked doors guarded by Dwayne and JP. So how did the shooter manage to slip through undetected? As if that wasn't enough for Humphrey to worry about, he also has an unexpected visit from his father (guest star James Fox), with whom he doesn't exactly see eye to eye

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Alexa investigates the hit-and-run death of an assistant to a PR mogul. Although the drunk driver involved turned herself in, Alexa believes the accident may be the work of an opportunistic killer. To her surprise, she finds a simple bus timetable holds the clue to whodunit - and why. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: A convicted paedophile is spotted taking photographs of children by Rebus and a close colleague, who apparently reacts badly to the incident as he turns up dead the next day. However, the distraught detective thinks there's more to it than plain suicide. He vows to get to the bottom of the mystery, but soon becomes distracted by a plea from an old girlfriend to find her missing teenage son. Gritty crime thriller, starring John Hannah, Mark Bonnar and Michelle Fairley

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The death of an insurance investigator from suspected cyanide poisoning results in Leo being investigated for fraud when the victim's over-zealous work replacement accuses him of falsifying a report. Meanwhile, Harry runs into an old colleague who turns out to be associated with the pathologist's predicament. William Gaminara and Tom Ward star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ricky Hanson faces trial for attempted murder, and his lawyer sets about trying to discredit each member of the squad. However, Jack receives a welcome distraction when an old friend asks him to reinvestigate the death of a wealthy businessman, and he, Brian and Gerry spring into action. Guest starring David Troughton, with Amanda Redman, James Bolam, Alun Armstrong and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: As Bayleaf recovers from the attack, a high-ranking officer arrives at the station to interview Nick about the prison incident. Pearce and his busybody wife Patti unwittingly find themselves the centre of attention at Recall's charity event for Great Ormond Street Hospital, and the hard-pressed Blue Watch firefighters are called to a blaze in a library basement. Ben Onwukwe, James Hazeldine, Ross Boatman and Glen Murphy star

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: An expectant mother on her way to hospital is involved in a major traffic accident, and Pearce finds himself acting as a midwife

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Laura Bryant is horrified to learn her son has been linked with a gang accused of terrorising an old man and there's further disappointment when he's brought in for questioning. Dan and Tony go speed dating and are shocked to find two female colleagues on the list. Phil's relationship with solicitor Heather Lees draws comments from Sam Nixon, while brother Steve is promised a send-off to remember

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Part two of two. Secrets and lies about Abra's past catch up with him, threatening Kyla's chances of getting Max back from social services. Maddy is terrified as detectives question her over Sunny's death but finds an ally in Dan who blackmails Ric over the secret night operations to prevent the police discovering the truth. James wants Mark to help him hide his drug habit from Elliot

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Comfort is racked with guilt after kissing Finlay, but her vow to stay away from temptation is soon swept aside when they are thrown together in a dangerous situation. Gilly storms out after Spencer admits he believes Anna's claims and Jack returns to the hospital. Drama, starring Kwame Kwei-Armah and Martina Laird

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: James Tennant reports that a local paper has taken a call from a man claiming to have his daughter Amy and an arrest soon follows. June's handling of three youths brought in for vandalism makes an impression on single headmaster Rod Jessop, and Mia gets Mickey Webb to open up about his past relationships. Tony gets involved in police work while moonlighting as a cabbie - to Dan Casper's amusement

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Everyone in Albert Square has been invited to Phil and Kathy's engagement at the Queen Vic. But when Grant gets some devastating news it becomes a party no-one will forget

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil and Kathy's party goes off with a bang. Underneath the Arches the blood starts to flow, and Sharon realises things can never be the same again.

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: The team continue to look for survivors after the explosion at the block of flats they were attending, as Sally and Coleman try to get Pearce on the radio

programme.desc: An old friend of Andrew arrives in Cardale with devastating news. Medical drama, starring Adrian Lukis and Saskia Wickham

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul links two rapes when both victims suffer symptoms of hepatitis, but bringing the case to court proves more difficult than expected

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey have a new sideline in double-glazing, and a new, ruthless saleswoman in Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's complaints of rising damp fall on deaf ears when Diana is notified her high-yield, high-risk securities are in receivership. Harvey makes a suggestion that he hopes will address both issues. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick is imprisoned in General Von Klinkerhoffen's dungeon, leaving Rene to rescue him, and there is a plan afoot to micro-photograph Hitler's invasion plans

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler comes face to face with the woman of his dreams and promptly sweeps her off her feet - on to the back of his tandem, and Barry resolves to throw caution to the wind and show his affection for Glenda. Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, starring Jean Alexander, Thora Hird and Stephen Lewis alongside stalwarts Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to re-create a Victorian garden in Regent's Park, where they meet all manner of intriguing characters - but the oasis of calm in central London is shattered by the discovery of a body. The victim is a respected Harley Street psychiatrist - whose three-legged dog appears to hold the key to solving the case. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ricky Hanson faces trial for attempted murder, and his lawyer sets about trying to discredit each member of the squad. programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo, Nikki and Jack travel to war-torn Afghanistan to identify whether human remains are those of a missing British soldier suspected of being captured by Taliban forces five years earlier. However, they soon discover there is more at stake than identifying a soldier's remains as the crime scene is preventing the completion of a charity-run sanitation plant, putting thousands of lives at risk from contaminated water. William Gaminara, Emilia Fox and David Caves star

programme.desc: Iwan Edwards praises the earwig, Sioned Edwards discusses the agapanthus, aka the African lily, and Meinir Gwilym shows how to repot standards

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Ffermio
programme.desc: Country and farming magazine programme, including features on all aspects of agriculture and rural life

programme.title: Rygbi: Clasuron Bermuda
programme.desc: Highlights from the 2019 World Rugby Bermuda Classic, when Delon Armitage was there to boost the Lions' efforts

programme.title: Côr Digidol Rhys Meirion
programme.desc: Rhys Meirion conducts a social experiment by bringing people from far and near to enjoy the thrill of singing together on a laptop in the kitchen, tablet in the shed or PC in the attic. Plus, the stories of the nurse who continues to work hard against the coronavirus, the scattered family, the internet music teacher and the vulnerable individuals trying to cope with the process of self-isolation

programme.title: Nyrsys
programme.desc: The programme visits Ysbyty Gwynedd Bangor and the Specialist Palliative Care Unit in Penglais

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on seafood, cooking monkfish with mussels with Fenella and Kirsty from the Seafood Shack in Ullapool, along with athlete JJ Chalmers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team hear plans for a flat in Bow in London, a terrace in Blaengwynfi in the Afan Valley and a three bed semi in Alvaston, Derbyshire

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Anthony McGill v Jamie Clarke in round two

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ethan entrusts Fenisha with the truth about his PTSD and the death of Big Al. Inspired by his faith in him, Ethan decides to give HEMS another try, and as the day winds down, Fenisha feels closer to Ethan than ever. Meanwhile, David struggles to manage the symptoms of his Bipolar disorder, and Robyn encourages him to step back from Matt. Elsewhere, as Faith prepares for Luka's first day of Proton Beam Therapy, Dylan's fury towards Lev builds. Starring George Rainsford, Jason Durr and Jacey Salles

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Thor: The Dark World (2013)
programme.desc: The alien thunder god returns to Earth when a powerful weapon from the dawn of time is discovered. The device attracts the attention of an old enemy of his people, who intends to use it to plunge the universe into darkness - and to stop him, Thor must form an uneasy alliance with his treacherous brother Loki. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Chris Hemsworth, Tom Hiddleston and Natalie Portman

programme.title: Behind the Candelabra (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of pianist Liberace, following his relationship with his younger lover Scott Thorson. Introduced into the flamboyant entertainer's hedonistic world at a Las Vegas show in 1977, Thorson began a tempestuous five-year love affair with Liberace, while maintaining the public illusion of simply being an employee. Directed by Steven Soderbergh and starring Michael Douglas and Matt Damon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Matthew and Zelease Ballentine-Brain fly out to Melbourne for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved to Australia permanently

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties featured are a one-bedroom flat in a former mill in Preston, a canalside cottage in Stourport-on-Severn in Worcestershire and a terraced house in Chiswick in west London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Colin Young and Jonathan Pratt help two teams look for items at an antiques fair at Newmarket racecourse, with Charlie Ross overseeing proceedings

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hears how Christian reformers founded the National Trust 125 years ago, and Bristol mayor Marvin Rees reflects on the city's links to slavery

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who have £500,000 to spend on a large family home in Northern Ireland where they can also keep animals and welcome paying guests. Away from the search, Nicki immerses herself in the country's maritime history at the dry dock where RMS Titanic was built

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team repurpose an old road light, a 1970s-style sofa, a worn-out dressing table and a set of moth-eaten blankets

programme.title: Tommy's Honour (2016)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama examining the relationship between the dynamic father-son team who ushered in the modern game of golf. Directed by Jason Connery, and starring Sam Neill, Peter Mullan, Jack Lowden and Ophelia Lovibond

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals often get up to mischief among themselves - and some are downright criminal. A colony of adelie penguins hunker down to avoid a storm, unaware one of them is a thief out to steal the stones that make up their nests, and Spy Tropicbird witnesses an aerial attack by aggressive frigate birds who attempt to steal his fish. A boisterous elephant has a temper tantrum in the mud near the waterhole, chimpanzees have extra-marital affairs and lemurs get high by sniffing toxic millipedes

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in the Cotswolds at the Broadway Tower folly to look back at unusual events that have featured on the programme over the years. Including the time Anita Rani met a farmer growing a crop of chairs, and when Margherita Taylor made microscopic art from nature

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Eltham Palace, and explores the art deco wing that was built in the 1930s. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of duelling pistols with a story to tell, two rare Fijian war clubs, and a panel of stained glass from the Houses of Parliament which was broken during the Blitz

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Concerned by Lata's growing friendship with poet Amit Chatterji, Mrs Mehra enlists the help of a friend to find her headstrong daughter a husband. When Rupa meets enthusiastic shoemaker Haresh Khanna she is immediately charmed, but Lata is not so easily convinced. Banished to the countryside with his Urdu tutor, Maan is preoccupied by thoughts of Saeeda Bai, but as his eyes are opened to the hardships of rural life, he becomes an unlikely campaigner for social justice.

programme.title: Saving Mr Banks (2013)
programme.desc: Walt Disney becomes determined to adapt Mary Poppins into a film, but faces an obstacle in the shape of author PL Travers, who resists the idea of a sentimental Hollywood version of her book. As the movie mogul's charm offensive comes unstuck, he begins to realise why the story is so important to its creator. Fact-based drama, with Emma Thompson, Tom Hanks, Paul Giamatti and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit cases previously featured to check on the progress of the investigations, beginning with the case of a wealthy female tenant poisoned by her landlord following a financial deal made shortly before her death in 1911

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Ashford in Kent, Egremont in Cumbria and Newport in South Wales

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help two teams find items at Detling Antiques Fair in Kent, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the origins of an underground seaside grotto

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Leonie tries to rectify the aftermath of a robbery, while it remains to be seen if Ana is invested in being with Evan

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Doctors stars Chris Walker, Matthew Chambers, Sarah Moyle, Ian Midlane and Elisabeth Dermot Walsh take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Dorset assisting two teachers - who have a £550,000 budget - find a country home where they can raise their young daughters

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of south-east England, along the way encountering items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Maisie Smith, Zack Morris and Lorraine Stanley, aka Tiffany Butcher, Keegan Baker and Karen Taylor, who chat about their biggest storylines and share backstage gossip and secrets from the set. Stacey also finds time to put in a shift at the nation's favourite launderette - where Lorraine lets her in on the inner workings of the machines and reveals what it was like to work with June Brown, aka Dot

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Pat's family make their annual pilgrimage to the site of his death and he begs Alison to pass on a message from the other side - a plan complicated when he realises a shocking truth about his marriage. Meanwhile, Mike struggles to come to terms with his bathroom-based paranoia, and Kitty tries to find out where babies come from. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Arnott is convinced Gates played a part in Jackie's disappearance, so the team uses Fleming to set a trap, hoping he will reveal his involvement in covering up the missing woman's crimes. Thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim has romance on his mind and has organised an elaborate date with Ire with the help of his housemate Emily, but things do not go entirely to plan. Jeremiah tries to rebuild his relationship with his estranged, while Simi is left holding the Speedi Kazz fort, but she may be about to fall into a very dangerous trap

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Comedian Tom Green hosts as the contestants' creative marketing skills are put to the test as they write and produce commercials for fictional drag queen law firms

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Former Labour home secretary Alan Johnson takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist and political commentator Tim Shipman joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Children plan weddings for their parents or stepparents

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce clips themed on marriage, including how a porcupine found true love in a zoo, and why a sewer may be a good place to prospect for gold

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends go to Herstmonceux Castle to take part in a medieval festival

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield come to the aid of Carrie, a 10-year-old from Leeds who plans to celebrate her red hair with a gingerbread house den

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve dives with hammerhead and tiger sharks, exploring how to save them from extinction.

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Ageing silverback leader Titus faces his greatest challenge when he tries to care for a vulnerable orphan, while hot-headed youngster Marembo sets about making his presence felt in the mountains of Uganda. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Martin Gould v Kyren Wilson, and Barry Hawkins v Neil Robertson

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares pork loin stuffed with oregano and Parma ham, served with mock mash and vignarola. She extols the virtues of spelt spaghetti, which she accompanies with an olive and anchovy sauce that can be made without cooking, and also demonstrates her recipe for sambuca kisses, a doughnut-like sweet

programme.title: The Good Cook
programme.desc: Food writer Simon Hopkinson demonstrates how to create restaurant-quality dishes at home, preparing classic chicken, Parmesan biscuits, pear salad, gravadlax and a rice pudding. He also discusses his favourite ingredients, continuing with salt and Roquefort cheese

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Spain on the final leg of their journey in search of Europe's finest baking. Travelling across the northern part of the country, they begin in St Sebastian, sampling delicacies including goose barnacles and pinchos - large tapas - and visiting a Basque dining club. Moving on, the duo bake dishes such as the Spanish bread pan rustico and a seafood empanada packed with locally caught fish. They also make a giant spelt sausage roll filled with chorizo, and join in with an ancient pagan ceremony in Galicia, before heading to Santiago de Compostela, where they end their 5,000-mile journey at a spectacular firework display to celebrate the 800th anniversary of St James

programme.title: The Dark Mirror (1946)
programme.desc: A doctor is murdered, and suspicion falls on his girlfriend. The police discover she has an identical twin, and each sister provides the other with an alibi. A psychiatrist is called in to determine which of the women is the killer, but he begins to fall for one of his suspects. Mystery, starring Olivia de Havilland in a dual role, alongside Lew Ayres and Thomas Mitchell

programme.title: Animals with Cameras
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan and a team of scientists learn more about various species through the use of animal bodycams. In the Atlantic, they reveal why a large number of devil rays gather near the Azores archipelago every summer. Footage of brown bears in north-east Turkey uncovers a fight between two males and a touching courtship scene, while in southern France, night-vision cameras are used to prove whether guardian dogs are an effective way of protecting sheep from wolf attacks

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Anthony McGill v Jamie Clarke in round two

programme.title: Jonathan Edwards - One Giant Leap
programme.desc: A celebration of the British athletics great's triple-jump world record, which saw him leap 18.29 metres at the 1995 World Championships in Gothenburg, Sweden

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims, beginning in the city of Orleans in north-central France. Here, he follows his Bradshaw's guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, which tell the story of the heroine of France, Joan of Arc. Later, Michael takes a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car, before making his way to the capital city of Paris. East of Paris, Michael finishes this leg of his journey in style with a tour of the cellars at Domaine Pommery in Reims

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fourth day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. The sides have been very evenly matched in this format over recent years, with the last two series between the nations ending in draws on English soil

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian reveals the myths and deceptions told following the United States' emergence as a superpower after the Second World War. The 1950s and early 1960s in America is remembered as a golden era of abundance, harmony and the American dream made real. Lucy Worsley reveals that to have been a carefully constructed illusion and that the era of America's supremacy was a time of government deception, racial conflict and fears of nuclear annihilation

programme.title: The Revenant (2015)
programme.desc: A frontiersman leading a hunting party through the wilderness is mauled by a bear, and a travelling companion who pledged to stay with him until help comes kills his son and leaves him for dead. Against all odds, the wounded man survives his injuries and embarks on a gruelling quest for revenge that takes him through a harsh landscape. Western, starring an Oscar-winning Leonardo DiCaprio, with Tom Hardy and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of this evening's second-round matches in Sheffield, featuring Martin Gould v Kyren Wilson, and Barry Hawkins v Neil Robertson

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights from this afternoon's second-round matches, which saw four players return to the tables for the second session of their ties

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler shows how to stock up vegetables for the colder months of the year as she grows courgettes, squashes, kale and Jerusalem artichokes. She also learns how to preserve food by using easy pickling recipes, forages for seeds and plants winter crops to keep her going until the next spring

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode reaches the letter V in his alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive, highlighting Anjum Anand's vindaloo recipe from Indian Food Made Easy, and Sophie Dahl's tips on baking a Victoria sponge

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don shows how to stake dahlias, sows seeds of perennials and celebrates an abundant vegetable harvest. The programme catches up with a landscape designer in Cornwall who creates gardens that are not only sustainable but also beautiful and edible, and travels to Wales to meet a woman whose garden reflects memories of her childhood home in Jamaica. Plus, memories of 2019, when Carol Klein visited Aberglasney Gardens in Carmarthenshire to look at their stunning collection of summer flowering thalictrum and Mark Lane revealed the adaptive tools he uses for planting and weeding

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George gives a greenhouse update and shows the benefits of late sown cucumbers. In Carole's countryside garden, she deals with vine weevil, a persistent garden pest

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites Tony Blackburn to take him back to the home his father designed in Poole, Dorset. The pair also visit the nearby beach where Tony used to sell ice-cream in the school holidays, and Bournemouth Pavilion where he learned the ropes as a performer and entertainer. Brian cooks a nostalgic dessert to remind Tony of his mother's cooking, and creates a culinary tribute - which puts the veteran disc jockey's life on a plate

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley joins a roller derby team ahead of a match, and Ashley John-Baptiste discovers how pushing yourself physically can lead to an increase in mental resilience

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Two more second-round matches are set to reach a conclusion at the Crucible Theatre - Martin Gould v Kyren Wilson, and Barry Hawkins v Neil Robertson. The second round last year produced several close finishes, none closer than Stephen Maguire's victory over James Cahill, which went to the 25th and deciding frame. Cahill had defeated Ronnie O'Sullivan in the first round, but couldn't quite make it to the quarter-finals, with Maguire progressing instead to a meeting with eventual champion Judd Trump. There were also exits for four former champions, with Mark Williams, Stuart Bingham, Shaun Murphy and Mark Selby all missing out on a place in the last eight

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The team looks back at highlights of the last two series of the motoring show, including Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness heading to the searing heat of Ethiopia in their first cars, and Chris racing the £2.5m McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's newest fighter jet

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The final session in the second round gets under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Anthony McGill v Jamie Clarke. Those involved will be hoping to win with something to spare this evening, as they'll be back in the arena tomorrow afternoon for the quarter-finals, with the first players to reach 13 frames progressing to the last eight

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fifth and final day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. The sides last met here in this format in 2016, when current captain Joe Root scored 254 in the first innings and added another 71 in the second as England recorded an emphatic 330-run victory

programme.title: The Australian Dream (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary profiling Australian Football League legend Adam Goodes, centring on an incident when he was racially abused by a 13-year-old opposition supporter. The film unpicks the unfolding events from 2013 to 2015, raising fundamental questions about the nature of racism and discrimination in society today

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claire tries to restore normality after a date takes a tragic turn, while Hiro convinces Ando they must travel to America to save the world. Matt is given a job with the FBI to help track down Sylar, a sinister force leads Niki into the desert, and Peter reveals his true feelings for Simone at a fundraising event for his brother. Starring Masi Oka, Sendhil Ramamurthy and Noah Gray-Cabey

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The heroes paths' cross in Las Vegas, where Hiro exploits his powers to help Ando win in a casino and Niki encounters Nathan. Meanwhile, Claire takes revenge on school quarterback Brody after their date and Matt's mind-reading abilities are tested by two men claiming to be experts in the field

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day 10 in Sheffield, where Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Anthony McGill v Jamie Clarke reached a conclusion

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of this afternoon's second-round matches at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, which were played over the best of 25 frames

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric's operation takes place on the same day as Guy's disciplinary hearing, forcing him to choose between treatment that could his life and his need to see Guy face the consequences of his actions. Sacha's silent treatment leaves Essie convinced he knows something about Frankie's disappearance, and Chloe tries her hand at Internet dating - only for one of her matches to be admitted as a patient

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Two youngsters are left in charge when the rest of the group heads off to forage for food. Flower gives birth to another litter, and outcast Tosca tries to rejoin the family

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile railway tour of South-east Asia reaches its southern most point in Indonesia, with stop-offs in Java and Jakarta among the highlights. Michael uncovers Indonesia's colonial past as part of a Dutch empire and its rich history as a port and centre of the spice trade, negotiates the city's infamous traffic, takes a ride on the newly launched MRT metro and dines like a local, on Jakarta's famous street food

programme.title: Westminster in Review
programme.desc: Alicia McCarthy looks back at how Westminster has tackled the Budget, Huawei, Black Lives Matter and domestic abuse and other major stories aside from the pandemic

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Some of the most vulnerable members of the species struggle to survive, including two orphans whose mothers were killed and a young female who is finding it difficult to deal with unfamiliar emotions. The new 'gorilla king' also has trouble keeping those in his care under control. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The quarter-finals get under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, with eight frames scheduled to take place in each match at the top and bottom of the draw

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Two more quarter-finals commence at the Crucible Theatre, as day 11 of the marathon tournament continues, with eight frames scheduled to take place on each table

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of Flower's new pups ventures out with the foragers for the first time sooner than anticipated, but learns a valuable lesson about living in the wild and the environment of the Kalahari

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage from Sheffield, as two quarter-finals continue. The players involved were in action this morning, and return to the tables to play the second of three sessions. Only one of the quarter-finals at this stage last year was won by the higher-seeded player, with eventual champion Judd Trump easing to a 13-6 victory over Stephen Maguire

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: Mark Gatiss, Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith explore Victoria Wood's take on TV. Clips include banal TV presenters Marjorie and Joan and Sally and Martin, Acorn Antiques and a pastiche of a 1960's episode of Coronation Street. The hosts also look at Victoria's pioneering mockumentaries

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The fifth first-round match sees four students from the University of Reading pit their wits against a team from Birkbeck, University of London in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the consequences of the 2003 invasion of Iraq by the US-led coalition, with America forced to return to confront the brutal legacy when Isis emerges to take over huge swathes of the country. Omar Mohmamed, a university professor from Mosul, recalls how the night armed men invaded his city, proclaiming they were the Islamic State. For the next three years, he lived under one of the most brutal regimes the world has ever known, witnessing daily executions, hand cutting and stonings

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Topical comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of guests Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Milton Jones, Zoe Lyons and Geoff Norcott. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on day 11, featuring the second sessions of two quarter-finals, with a further eight frames scheduled on each table

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights from this afternoon's quarter-finals, as four players took to the tables at the Crucible Theatre once again to get their matches under way

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: The three artists travel to a home in Birmingham, hoping to assist a buyer born on the weekend of the moon landings, who wants to mark her 50th birthday year with something out of this world

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Martin Gould v Kyren Wilson, and Barry Hawkins v Neil Robertson in the second round. One of the best matches of this stage last year saw four-time world champion John Higgins overcome 2015 title-winner Stuart Bingham 13-11, and the 'Wizard of Wilshaw' went on to reach another final, but eventually met his match against Judd Trump

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The drone launch appears to have been successful, albeit at a terrible cost, but just as life in Brizan is beginning to return to a sense of normality, Thomas spots the cloud again. French supernatural drama, starring Gael Raes

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The authorities put Brizan into quarantine and everyone now realises that Ben and Faust have been right all along in making the connection between the cloud and the strange events that have followed its appearance. Then, Ben works out that the solution to the crisis must come from the surfers themselves. In French

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry

programme.title: Everything: The Real Thing Story
programme.desc: The rise of four working class boys from one of Liverpool's toughest neighbourhoods, who fought hardship and discrimination to become the biggest-selling black group in British pop history. With a string of hits, they dominated the international charts throughout the 1970s with iconic songs like You to Me Are Everything, Can't Get by Without You, and Can You Feel the Force. Hpwever the group's meteoric success was also tempered with personal tragedy, drug addiction and racial prejudice. Narrated by Jacob Anderson

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the records that dominated the year, featuring songs by Bon Jovi, Cyndi Lauper, Cameo, the Pretenders, the Real Thing, Billy Ocean and the Communards. Other highlights include the Housemartins, Kim Wilde, the Human League and the Cure

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Portrait of a Lifetime
programme.desc: A programme celebrating Billy Connolly's 75th birthday and 50 years in the business, as three Scottish artists, John Byrne, Jack Vettriano and Rachel Maclean, each create a new portrait of the comedian and actor. As he sits with each artist, Billy talks about his remarkable career which has taken him from musician and stand-up to Hollywood star and national treasure

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: Colin Morgan narrates a documentary following life on Upper and Lower Lough Erne over the course of the four seasons, beginning with spring. Retired school teacher Pat Lunny takes a trip out on his boat around his usual haunts around the islands near his home town of Enniskillen, while across the lough on White North Island, Amy Burns from RSPBNI is moving Highland cattle to some of the charity's other reserves

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces a performance from 2002, with Simon Rattle conducting the National Youth Orchestra of Great Britain in Mahler's Eighth Symphony, with five choirs and soloists including Christine Brewer, John Relyea and Jane Henschel joining them on stage

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The final session in the second round continues at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Anthony McGill v Jamie Clarke

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and Maggie Aderin-Pocock revisit the BBC's archive in a programme dedicated to humanity's ongoing fascination with the planet Mars

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The world's leading experts discuss where they would go on Mars, if they got the chance, and what they would need to survive on the planet. In the ultimate traveller's guide to Mars and using real images and data, Horizon brings Martian landmarks to life, from vast plains and towering volcanoes to deep valleys and hidden underground caverns, and also show where to land, where to live and even where to hunt for traces of extraterrestrial life. Narrated by Mark Gatiss

programme.title: Mars Uncovered: Ancient God of War
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes investigates the enduring relationship between warfare and worship by following the trail of the ancient Roman god of war Mars. She begins in Carthage, site of one of the most significant and bloodiest victories in the history of the Roman Empire and explores the vital role of Mars in Rome's Imperial expansion. Bettany also considers how the figure of Mars was used by artists such as Botticelli and Rubens to examine the inevitability of war, and whether the benefits of war still outweigh the horrors

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: Colin Morgan narrates a documentary following life on Upper and Lower Lough Erne over the course of the four seasons, beginning with spring. Retired school teacher Pat Lunny takes a trip out on his boat around his usual haunts around the islands near his home town of Enniskillen, while across the lough on White North Island, Amy Burns from RSPBNI is moving Highland cattle to some of the charity's other reserves

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Christian disagrees with Roxy's decision and warns her she'll have to face the inevitable when Jack and Sean find out. The Masoods' financial problems force Zainab to take desperate action and Patrick plans a birthday surprise for Chelsea. Darren and Libby have relationship troubles

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tanya and Jack grow closer, but an enraged Sean warns her off. Roxy is horrified when Jean invites her to a family lunch and sets out to give her a piece of her mind. Chelsea can't hide her disappointment when a surprise guest arrives at her birthday celebration, and Heather's dreams are shattered

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sean terrorises Tanya in her own home, while Roxy makes plans to escape Walford to avoid his interference. Masood sets about finding a solution to the family's financial crisis, which prompts Zainab to implement changes of her own. Minty tries to make amends after upsetting Heather

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: After a terrifying journey Jean helps Sean make a decision about the baby, while Roxy is determined to keep the truth from Jack and Ronnie. Darren and Libby are unaware their intimate conversation has been overheard, and Heather finds a way to officially end her marriage to Minty

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: In early summer, mayfly begin to hatch and fisherman Michael Shortt is fly fishing for trout. Amy Burns from RSPB NI travels to island reserves to install cameras and measure eggs

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene of bright green grassy forest, through which a smooth crystal river flows quietly

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further quarter-final coverage from Sheffield, featuring the second sessions of two matches, with the players needing to win 13 frames to progress to the semi-finals

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, where he learns how the late 19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria. Moving on, the presenter also confronts his fear of snakes to assist in a surgical operation on a venomous king cobra and steps into the ring to learn Muay Thai boxing

programme.title: Treasures of the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez investigates the development of Anglo-Saxon art. Examining the Sutton Hoo treasures the Franks Casket, the Staffordshire Hoard and the Lindisfarne Gospels, she uncovers the secret codes and symbols that reveal the pagan past and Christian future of the Anglo-Saxon people, and discusses how their artistic development was brought to an end by the Norman invasion of 1066

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the stories of sculptors John Flaxman, Francis Chantrey and Alfred Gilbert, who bucked trends in the 18th and 19th centuries. He examines the technical breakthroughs behind their key works - such as carving in marble with a pointer machine and the lost-wax technique - which were produced at a time of increased interest in sculptural projects to celebrate the size of the British Empire

programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.
