programme.desc: Baby Soup Dragon is upset because his mother will not let him use her big ladle to help make the soup, so Tiny and Small tell him he can give them a hand instead

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos play with the ball indoors, but it flies out of the window

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: It Takes Two To T'Wit T'Woo by Paula Knight is the tale of a lonely owl looking for a new friend. Read by Isla Fisher

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on seafood, cooking monkfish with mussels with Fenella and Kirsty from the Seafood Shack in Ullapool, along with athlete JJ Chalmers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team hear plans for a flat in Bow in London, a terrace in Blaengwynfi in the Afan Valley and a three bed semi in Alvaston, Derbyshire

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Ronnie O'Sullivan and Anthony McGill v Jamie Clarke in round two

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Boxer Carl Frampton, journalist Victoria Fritz, dancer Wayne Sleep and TV presenter Rav Wilding take to the black chair, answering questions on The Office, the Chelsea Flower Show, theatrical producer Lilian Baylis and The Inbetweeners

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ethan entrusts Fenisha with the truth about his PTSD and the death of Big Al. Inspired by his faith in him, Ethan decides to give HEMS another try, and as the day winds down, Fenisha feels closer to Ethan than ever. Meanwhile, David struggles to manage the symptoms of his Bipolar disorder, and Robyn encourages him to step back from Matt. Elsewhere, as Faith prepares for Luka's first day of Proton Beam Therapy, Dylan's fury towards Lev builds. Starring George Rainsford, Jason Durr and Jacey Salles

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy seems reluctant to reveal any information about her new boyfriend, Mark refuses to talk about why his wife has thrown him out, and Dermot lies to his fiancee's parents about his job - all of which makes Agnes feel her family is becoming too secretive. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Thor: The Dark World (2013)
programme.desc: The alien thunder god returns to Earth when a powerful weapon from the dawn of time is discovered. The device attracts the attention of an old enemy of his people, who intends to use it to plunge the universe into darkness - and to stop him, Thor must form an uneasy alliance with his treacherous brother Loki. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Chris Hemsworth, Tom Hiddleston and Natalie Portman

programme.title: Behind the Candelabra (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of pianist Liberace, following his relationship with his younger lover Scott Thorson. Introduced into the flamboyant entertainer's hedonistic world at a Las Vegas show in 1977, Thorson began a tempestuous five-year love affair with Liberace, while maintaining the public illusion of simply being an employee. Directed by Steven Soderbergh and starring Michael Douglas and Matt Damon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Matthew and Zelease Ballentine-Brain fly out to Melbourne for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved to Australia permanently

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties featured are a one-bedroom flat in a former mill in Preston, a canalside cottage in Stourport-on-Severn in Worcestershire and a terraced house in Chiswick in west London

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen embarks on a quest to discover the truth about the human body. It's a story of breakdancers, contortionists and '80s jacuzzis, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hears how Christian reformers founded the National Trust 125 years ago, and Bristol mayor Marvin Rees reflects on the city's links to slavery

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: A look back at the 2001 Six Nations match between France and Wales at the Stade de France

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tracks wildlife in the Cambrian mountains, the remote uplands that form the heart and spine of Wale. His first target is the elusive pine marten, finding one at night lurking in a conifer forest, and Iolo also looks for ospreys fishing on Llyn Clywedog

programme.title: Tommy's Honour (2016)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama examining the relationship between the dynamic father-son team who ushered in the modern game of golf. Directed by Jason Connery, and starring Sam Neill, Peter Mullan, Jack Lowden and Ophelia Lovibond

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards continues his exploration of the country's history by examining how, in 1485, Henry VII returned from exile in France to capture the English crown, becoming the first Welsh king of England. He discovers how the two countries became united under his leadership, leaving huge consequences for future generations, and explains the significance of being a landowner in the years that followed. The programme uses computer graphics and dramatic reconstructions to bring some of Wales' most historic moments to life

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in the Cotswolds at the Broadway Tower folly to look back at unusual events that have featured on the programme over the years. Including the time Anita Rani met a farmer growing a crop of chairs, and when Margherita Taylor made microscopic art from nature

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Eltham Palace, and explores the art deco wing that was built in the 1930s. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of duelling pistols with a story to tell, two rare Fijian war clubs, and a panel of stained glass from the Houses of Parliament which was broken during the Blitz

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Concerned by Lata's growing friendship with poet Amit Chatterji, Mrs Mehra enlists the help of a friend to find her headstrong daughter a husband. When Rupa meets enthusiastic shoemaker Haresh Khanna she is immediately charmed, but Lata is not so easily convinced. Banished to the countryside with his Urdu tutor, Maan is preoccupied by thoughts of Saeeda Bai, but as his eyes are opened to the hardships of rural life, he becomes an unlikely campaigner for social justice.

programme.title: Saving Mr Banks (2013)
programme.desc: Walt Disney becomes determined to adapt Mary Poppins into a film, but faces an obstacle in the shape of author PL Travers, who resists the idea of a sentimental Hollywood version of her book. As the movie mogul's charm offensive comes unstuck, he begins to realise why the story is so important to its creator. Fact-based drama, with Emma Thompson, Tom Hanks, Paul Giamatti and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit cases previously featured to check on the progress of the investigations, beginning with the case of a wealthy female tenant poisoned by her landlord following a financial deal made shortly before her death in 1911

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Ashford in Kent, Egremont in Cumbria and Newport in South Wales

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help two teams find items at Detling Antiques Fair in Kent, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the origins of an underground seaside grotto

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Leonie tries to rectify the aftermath of a robbery, while it remains to be seen if Ana is invested in being with Evan

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Doctors stars Chris Walker, Matthew Chambers, Sarah Moyle, Ian Midlane and Elisabeth Dermot Walsh take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Dorset assisting two teachers - who have a £550,000 budget - find a country home where they can raise their young daughters

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of south-east England, along the way encountering items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Maisie Smith, Zack Morris and Lorraine Stanley, aka Tiffany Butcher, Keegan Baker and Karen Taylor, who chat about their biggest storylines and share backstage gossip and secrets from the set. Stacey also finds time to put in a shift at the nation's favourite launderette - where Lorraine lets her in on the inner workings of the machines and reveals what it was like to work with June Brown, aka Dot

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Gwent Police's dedicated forensic collision investigators as they deal with serious incidents on the roads. In this edition, a hit-and-run leaves a cyclist fighting for her life, while mystery surrounds the death of a delivery driver on a busy dual carriageway

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Arnott is convinced Gates played a part in Jackie's disappearance, so the team uses Fleming to set a trap, hoping he will reveal his involvement in covering up the missing woman's crimes. Thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim has romance on his mind and has organised an elaborate date with Ire with the help of his housemate Emily, but things do not go entirely to plan. programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals often get up to mischief among themselves - and some are downright criminal. A colony of adelie penguins hunker down to avoid a storm, unaware one of them is a thief out to steal the stones that make up their nests, and Spy Tropicbird witnesses an aerial attack by aggressive frigate birds who attempt to steal his fish. A boisterous elephant has a temper tantrum in the mud near the waterhole, chimpanzees have extra-marital affairs and lemurs get high by sniffing toxic millipedes

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team repurpose an old road light, a 1970s-style sofa, a worn-out dressing table and a set of moth-eaten blankets

programme.title: Tommy's Honour (2016)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama examining the relationship between the dynamic father-son team who ushered in the modern game of golf. Directed by Jason Connery, and starring Sam Neill, Peter Mullan, Jack Lowden and Ophelia Lovibond

programme.title: Tide
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at people who harness the waves for different purposes. Featuring seaweed farming, fishing with a traditional wooden mud horse, tidal power technology, an art installation, and a swimming challenge that uses the tide as a catapult. Plus, a look at how the largest passenger aircraft in the world is affected by tidal movements

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in the Cotswolds at the Broadway Tower folly to look back at unusual events that have featured on the programme over the years. Including the time Anita Rani met a farmer growing a crop of chairs, and when Margherita Taylor made microscopic art from nature

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Eltham Palace, and explores the art deco wing that was built in the 1930s. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of duelling pistols with a story to tell, two rare Fijian war clubs, and a panel of stained glass from the Houses of Parliament which was broken during the Blitz

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Concerned by Lata's growing friendship with poet Amit Chatterji, Mrs Mehra enlists the help of a friend to find her headstrong daughter a husband. When Rupa meets enthusiastic shoemaker Haresh Khanna she is immediately charmed, but Lata is not so easily convinced. Banished to the countryside with his Urdu tutor, Maan is preoccupied by thoughts of Saeeda Bai, but as his eyes are opened to the hardships of rural life, he becomes an unlikely campaigner for social justice.

programme.title: Hume
programme.desc: A profile of former Irish politician John Hume, whose career began in the civil rights movement and includes the winning of the 1998 Nobel Peace Prize in conjunction with David Trimble. With contributions by Bono, Tony Blair, and Hume himself

programme.title: Saving Mr Banks (2013)
programme.desc: Walt Disney becomes determined to adapt Mary Poppins into a film, but faces an obstacle in the shape of author PL Travers, who resists the idea of a sentimental Hollywood version of her book. As the movie mogul's charm offensive comes unstuck, he begins to realise why the story is so important to its creator. Fact-based drama, with Emma Thompson, Tom Hanks, Paul Giamatti and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit cases previously featured to check on the progress of the investigations, beginning with the case of a wealthy female tenant poisoned by her landlord following a financial deal made shortly before her death in 1911

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned in Ashford in Kent, Egremont in Cumbria and Newport in South Wales

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours sampling some of the US's most dangerous and demanding jobs, beginning by working as a correctional officer in a Texas jail

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help two teams find items at Detling Antiques Fair in Kent, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the origins of an underground seaside grotto

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Leonie tries to rectify the aftermath of a robbery, while it remains to be seen if Ana is invested in being with Evan

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Doctors stars Chris Walker, Matthew Chambers, Sarah Moyle, Ian Midlane and Elisabeth Dermot Walsh take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Dorset assisting two teachers - who have a £550,000 budget - find a country home where they can raise their young daughters

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich pick their favourite transformational makeovers, including turning a concrete backyard into a piece of paradise

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton take a tour of south-east England, along the way encountering items including a potter's wheel, before an auction in Tring, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps design a garden for Nichola McGregor in Co Antrim, who wants to bring a bit of France to her garden in memory of her late partner. He is joined by exotic plant expert Conrad McCormick, and shows how to rejuvenate a pond

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Maisie Smith, Zack Morris and Lorraine Stanley, aka Tiffany Butcher, Keegan Baker and Karen Taylor, who chat about their biggest storylines and share backstage gossip and secrets from the set. Stacey also finds time to put in a shift at the nation's favourite launderette - where Lorraine lets her in on the inner workings of the machines and reveals what it was like to work with June Brown, aka Dot

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Tom Heap investigates the impact of the lockdown due to the coronavirus pandemic on Britain's bfood manufacturing sector, and looks at the transformation in the way people shop, cook and consume food

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Arnott is convinced Gates played a part in Jackie's disappearance, so the team uses Fleming to set a trap, hoping he will reveal his involvement in covering up the missing woman's crimes. Thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Northern Ireland on The One Show
programme.desc: Jennie Browne and Joe Lindsay present more films from the magazine programme that were made in and about Northern Ireland, featuring reports on the bridge to Scotland and Enniskillen bog butter

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: Kazim has romance on his mind and has organised an elaborate date with Ire with the help of his housemate Emily, but things do not go entirely to plan. Jeremiah tries to rebuild his relationship with his estranged, while Simi is left holding the Speedi Kazz fort, but she may be about to fall into a very dangerous trap

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Comedian Tom Green hosts as the contestants' creative marketing skills are put to the test as they write and produce commercials for fictional drag queen law firms

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Former Labour home secretary Alan Johnson takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist and political commentator Tim Shipman joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Coverage of the response in the Scottish Parliament to the Summer Economic Statement update, from Thursday 9 July.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur interviews one of the UK's best-loved and most successful actors, Brian Cox. He is currently in New York, the US city worst affected by the coronavirus pandemic.

programme.desc: Conservative MP for Broxtowe, Darren Henry, gives his maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Thursday 26 June.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Some are predicting that despite the easing of the lockdown, the arts will be devastated by the financial impact of the pandemic. What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.title: Dino Dana

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo comes across Fizz encased in ice and thinks he has found a prehistoric man. He thaws him out and acts like a caveman, so as not to distress him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's helping out at an understaffed veterinary clinic. In fact, he's at the supermarket, where Fizz is trying to sell his cleaning products.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Kayo rides aboard Fireflash, a Brains-invented supersonic airliner, but is unaware its control has been seized by a mystery individual. Meanwhile, International Rescue realises the plane has vanished from its monitors and tries to locate it. After noticing the aircraft is flying in the wrong direction, Kayo manages to avoid a powerful gas that has knocked out both the passengers and crew

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tests out Fizz's latest invention, a three-piece swimsuit to make beachgoers look chic. Magoo mistakes him for the President.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz wants to cure Magoo's near-sightedness for good. He and Weasel go over to his house, only to be taken for Magoo's parents on a surprise visit.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced to spend the day outside in his doghouse due to Grandma Mikkelsen's fur allergy

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen

programme.title: Twins (1988)
programme.desc: An unlikely pair of brothers, who were born as the result of a one-off genetic experiment and separated at birth, are reunited in adulthood. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Danny DeVito, Kelly Preston and Chloe Webb

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis. A team of five from Holyhead take on the Tenable Tower as they attempt to win a £125,000 prize. Can they score a Perfect 10 to bring home the money?

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Thingamajig, Fox, Tree and Butterfly perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mikey North, Una Healy, Carl Froch and Alison Hammond pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the first episode, Stephen is joined by Coronation Street stars Jenni McAlpine, Antony Cotton and Sair Khan

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the beginning of this year's Battle Rounds. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are 12 places in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Spy Who Loved Me (1977)
programme.desc: James Bond teams up with a KGB agent to recover missing British and Soviet nuclear submarines, before whoever stole them can use their warheads. Unfortunately, 007 was responsible for the death of his new ally's lover - and she is determined to make him pay before their mission is complete. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Barbara Bach, Curt Jurgens and Richard Kiel

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Holly Willoughby welcomes footballer Ashley Cole, Strictly Come Dancing 2016 winner Ore Oduba and comedian Kevin Bridges to the show. They join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh as well as regular panellist Romesh Ranganathan and scorekeeper Seann Walsh

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Dermot O'Leary, in which teams of three must take on challenges in miniature settings to win a cash prize of up to £150,000. The first episode's contestants are members of the England amputee football team

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: As Fizz gets ready to travel to another dimension populated entirely by other Fizzes, Magoo turns up and thinks that Weasel has a new kitten.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for billionaire Bruce Payne, aka the superhero, Ratman. When Ratman is kidnapped, Magoo decides to save the day.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tends to Fizz's toothache, but Magoo turns up, thinking he's busting into an animal testing lab.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The Mikkelsen's house has been cleaned and the floor has been polished to a deep sheen. Mike has been told to keep his paws off it. Unfortunately, the filthy raccoons sneak in to steal from the house and wreak havoc. Mercury is loving it, taking advantage of the shiny new floor to practice his ice skating. Mike is determined to get the raccoons out of the house without setting a single paw on the floor

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike decides to make tacos for Iris, but Fluffy and Turtles do everything they can to stop him

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The buffoon is delighted to meet a man with exactly the same tastes as himself - but jealousy soon rears its ugly head. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson's thrift store shopping is ruined

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. This morning they are joined by actor Stephen Tompkinson, McFly star and The Voice Kids coach Danny Jones and ITV presenter Ranvir Singh

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a menu perfect for enjoying inside and out. Dishes featured include spiced beer can chicken with saffron potatoes, homemade crisps and dips, summer cocktails, gammon steak, celeriac remoulade and coconut and polenta cake with honey syrup

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The baker makes rosemary and parmesan shortbreads, and charcoal Christmas tree crackers for cheese. Plus, Paul A Young makes his rum butter chocolate brownies

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three more factories producing some of the nation's favourite food-and-drink items. It reveals how millions of Drumstick lollipops are made at the Swizzels sweet factory in Derbyshire, and how the Mackie family produces its famous ice-cream in Aberdeen. There is also a look behind the scenes at the Robinsons squash factory, where orange cordial destined for the Centre Court of Wimbledon is produced. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the beginning of this year's Battle Rounds. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are 12 places in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Thingamajig, Fox, Tree and Butterfly perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the first episode, Stephen is joined by Coronation Street stars Jenni McAlpine, Antony Cotton and Sair Khan

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look back at the soap's scandalous storylines, with illicit affairs, teenage pregnancy and bigamy having all have rocked the street over the years. Among the stories featured are Sarah Louise Platt's teenage pregnancy and the Baldwin family's tangled affairs

programme.title: Moonraker (1979)
programme.desc: James Bond's investigations into the mysterious disappearance of a space shuttle lead him to a sinister industrialist, who is plotting to wipe out most of the human race. The secret agent also faces a rematch with his old enemy, metal-toothed hitman Jaws. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Michael Lonsdale, Richard Kiel and Lois Chiles

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family prepares for a celebration, and Margo is determined to persuade the reclusive Countess Mavrodaki to attend. Sven is put to the test by his potential family, but seems to make a good impression. Gerry begins to question whether keeping animals in his personal zoo is fair, and considers releasing them into the wild

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The Crawleys watch Henry compete in a motor racing event, an occasion that leaves Mary reeling. Violet hatches a plan which surprises the family and delights Miss Denker, and learns more about Amelia's intentions in befriending Isobel. Molesley and Daisy rise to the challenges of examination day, Andy's secret is brought to light, Thomas feels isolated, and Mrs Patmore and Mrs Hughes teach Carson a lesson

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Bertie's circumstances change suddenly, leaving Edith uncertain whether to risk telling him the truth or keep her past a secret at all costs. Mary wonders if she can ever make peace with her sister as hostilities escalate, Spratt shows some hidden talents and Molesley's first day as a teacher does not go according to plan. Mrs Patmore's bed and breakfast attracts unwanted attention, leaving her in need of the Crawleys' help

programme.title: What the Durrells Did Next
programme.desc: Keeley Hawes presents a look at the true story behind the drama series, revealing what the four Durrell siblings and their mother did when their time living on Corfu came to an end. The programme reveals stories of African expeditions, international celebrity, and the establishment of a zoo dedicated to saving endangered species

programme.title: Gone to Pot: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Linda Robson, Pam St Clement, Christopher Biggins, John Fashanu and Bobby George reach Colorado, home to the famous Rocky Mountains and one of the very first States to legalise the recreational use of marijuana. In Denver, Bobby, Fash and Linda pay their respects at a very different type of church - the International Church of Cannabis. Also on their tour itinerary is a look inside The Farm, a state-licensed marijuana operation, which boasts legalised cultivation of the drug on a mind-blowing scale

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues to explore the 870-mile Wales Coast Path. This time around, he travels down Cardigan Bay, where he helps monitor the local dolphin population, discovers a 4,000-year-old sunken forest on a beach and takes part in a Celtic Longboat rowing race

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts are in Bradford, where Tim Hogarth has his eye on a silver sculpture of a stag and Michael Hogben's finds include a wooden portrait and a gold pocket watch

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five drag artists from London answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Euen, Peter, Chanelle and Linda pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. And Karen the social worker promises Toyah she will put the fostering wheels in motion once she's received the reference from Leanne

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap 1970s-themed weekender, but Tracey is about to receive some eye-opening news that could spoil her fun. First of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: The soap was founded on its dominating matriarchs and strong northern working-class woman and this edition celebrates the best of them - including the likes of Ena Sharples, Ivy Tilsley, Vera Duckworth, Blanche Hunt and Evelyn Plummer. Comedian Jason Manford narrates the programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The first of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Oliver catches his teenage daughter Justina sneaking out of the house to attend a BLM march as he fears for her safety and is concerned she's putting herself at risk. Starring Nicholas Pinnock and Yasmin Monet Prince

programme.title: The Parachute Murder Plot
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce examines the case of Emile Cilliers, who was convicted in 2018 of the attempted murder of his wife Victoria, having tampered with her parachute before a routine skydive. He also deliberately caused a gas leak at the family home. With access to hours of police tapes, thousands of the couple's intimate text messages, unseen home movies and interviews with key people - some of whom are speaking publicly for the first time - Fiona goes beyond the headlines, shedding new light on the crime itself, the investigation and the subsequent prosecution

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress introduces more unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, this time focusing on her journeys to India and Uzbekistan. Joanna shares some previously unseen moments and provides viewers with an insight into the making of the series. In this episode, she travels the length of India, including footage of her encounters with the flower-sellers of Madurai, before taking us back to Uzbekistan, where she reveals some gory tales of the country's history and discovers the secrets of gem-making

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces more clips and profiles from the ITV sport archives, including a look at boxing star Nicola Adams, the story of rugby resilience in the Japanese city of Kamaishi, and golfer Seve Ballesteros playing for the cameras in the company of Jimmy Greaves

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to parathas, showcasing her own recipe for potato and cauliflower parathas served with kuchumber and introducing a report by food blogger Shashank Agarwal on an area of Delhi famous for the flatbreads. Parveen offers tips on different types of flour and prepares samosas with a spicy potato and pea filling

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dad tries to get Dana up and ready for school, but she is busy dreaming about discovering the first T Rex fossil

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he's hunting down a rat in his attic, Magoo finds himself on the theme park, devoted to... the glory of Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo comes across Fizz encased in ice and thinks he has found a prehistoric man. He thaws him out and acts like a caveman, so as not to distress him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's helping out at an understaffed veterinary clinic. In fact, he's at the supermarket, where Fizz is trying to sell his cleaning products.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Kayo rides aboard Fireflash, a Brains-invented supersonic airliner, but is unaware its control has been seized by a mystery individual. Meanwhile, International Rescue realises the plane has vanished from its monitors and tries to locate it. After noticing the aircraft is flying in the wrong direction, Kayo manages to avoid a powerful gas that has knocked out both the passengers and crew

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tests out Fizz's latest invention, a three-piece swimsuit to make beachgoers look chic. Magoo mistakes him for the President.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz wants to cure Magoo's near-sightedness for good. He and Weasel go over to his house, only to be taken for Magoo's parents on a surprise visit.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced to spend the day outside in his doghouse due to Grandma Mikkelsen's fur allergy

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is ecstatic to learn his favourite singer is performing at the London Palladium and moves heaven and Earth to get her to sign his autograph book. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Wet Wet Wet singer Marti Pellow, chefs Brian Turner and Cherish Finden, and seafood expert Dan the Fish Man

programme.title: Twins (1988)
programme.desc: An unlikely pair of brothers, who were born as the result of a one-off genetic experiment and separated at birth, are reunited in adulthood. Also on their tour itinerary is a look inside The Farm, a state-licensed marijuana operation, which boasts legalised cultivation of the drug on a mind-blowing scale

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues to explore the 870-mile Wales Coast Path. This time around, he travels down Cardigan Bay, where he helps monitor the local dolphin population, discovers a 4,000-year-old sunken forest on a beach and takes part in a Celtic Longboat rowing race

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts are in Bradford, where Tim Hogarth has his eye on a silver sculpture of a stag and Michael Hogben's finds include a wooden portrait and a gold pocket watch

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five drag artists from London answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Euen, Peter, Chanelle and Linda pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. And Karen the social worker promises Toyah she will put the fostering wheels in motion once she's received the reference from Leanne

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap 1970s-themed weekender, but Tracey is about to receive some eye-opening news that could spoil her fun. First of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: The soap was founded on its dominating matriarchs and strong northern working-class woman and this edition celebrates the best of them - including the likes of Ena Sharples, Ivy Tilsley, Vera Duckworth, Blanche Hunt and Evelyn Plummer. Comedian Jason Manford narrates the programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The first of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Oliver catches his teenage daughter Justina sneaking out of the house to attend a BLM march as he fears for her safety and is concerned she's putting herself at risk. Starring Nicholas Pinnock and Yasmin Monet Prince

programme.title: The Parachute Murder Plot
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce examines the case of Emile Cilliers, who was convicted in 2018 of the attempted murder of his wife Victoria, having tampered with her parachute before a routine skydive. He also deliberately caused a gas leak at the family home. With access to hours of police tapes, thousands of the couple's intimate text messages, unseen home movies and interviews with key people - some of whom are speaking publicly for the first time - Fiona goes beyond the headlines, shedding new light on the crime itself, the investigation and the subsequent prosecution

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress introduces more unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, this time focusing on her journeys to India and Uzbekistan. Joanna shares some previously unseen moments and provides viewers with an insight into the making of the series. In this episode, she travels the length of India, including footage of her encounters with the flower-sellers of Madurai, before taking us back to Uzbekistan, where she reveals some gory tales of the country's history and discovers the secrets of gem-making

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces more clips and profiles from the ITV sport archives, including a look at boxing star Nicola Adams, the story of rugby resilience in the Japanese city of Kamaishi, and golfer Seve Ballesteros playing for the cameras in the company of Jimmy Greaves

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen Ashraf presents a guide to parathas, showcasing her own recipe for potato and cauliflower parathas served with kuchumber and introducing a report by food blogger Shashank Agarwal on an area of Delhi famous for the flatbreads. Parveen offers tips on different types of flour and prepares samosas with a spicy potato and pea filling

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The body of Dr Stephen Black is found on an Oxford tour bus, and the detectives are led to sprawling Crevecoeur Hall, where Hathaway spent much of his childhood. As he investigates, he is drawn to Scarlett Mortmaigne, the glamorous daughter of the estate's owner, but when another corpse is discovered, Hathaway's refusal to believe the family is involved threatens to test his relationship with Lewis to breaking point. Guest starring Nathaniel Parker (The Inspector Lynley Mysteries), Juliet Aubrey (Primeval) and Stella Gonet (Holby City), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star

When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.desc: Sally implores Tim to accept that Elaine could be telling the truth and Geoff could have fed him a pack of lies all his life. Elsewhere, Leanne is overwhelmed with grief, and when Carla lets slip that Gary's binned his threat of a rent increase, Maria's concerned

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff swears that Elaine is peddling lies, she's not Tim's Mum and Alya must have put her up to it in a bid to drive a wedge between them. Leanne urges Toyah to set the fostering wheels in motion as she deserves some happiness. And Adam seethes when Sarah fails to mention Gary's change of heart over the rent

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Priya confronts Sarah, Harriet is stopped in her tracks, and Dan lashes out at Amelia

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Tracy receive surprising news, Priya decides to confront Al, and Gabby is feeling fed-up

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy and Nate have a difficult conversation, Nicola feels for Gabby, and Sam lets slip private information about Lydia

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Ever since a beautiful American woman arrived in the peaceful village of Wychwood-under-Ashe, people have begun to die in a series of apparent accidents. Nobody suspects a killer is at work but, as the body count rises, Miss Marple digs a little deeper with the help of a former policeman - and they gradually unearth a secret that will rock the village to its core. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, Benedict Cumberbatch, David Haig, Sylvia Syms, Margo Stilley and Hugo Speer

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of four races from Salisbury, including the 3.35 Sovereign Stakes, plus two handicaps from Thirsk, and the Phoenix Stakes from The Curragh. With analysis from Jason Weaver, Matt Chapman and Natalie Green, and commentary by Mark Johnson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The team assists 39-year-old mother of three Miriam Aragon in her search for her Salvadoran father. After growing up in Wales with her single mother and only finding out about her father's identity at the age of 19, Miriam is desperate to find him. Meanwhile, 48-year-old Annie Sims is determined to find her birth mother, who gave her up for adoption as a baby, but kept her older sister. Following the birth of her own daughters, Annie's compassion for her biological mother has grown, and she hopes to find her and hear her side of the story. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress flies over Tokyo in a helicopter. The city was bombed extensively during the Second World War, so almost all of it is a symbol of the post-war economic boom that saw Japan become the world's second largest economy. While in the capital, Joanna heads out to a nightclub to see a Japanese girl band and witnesses the largely male audience perform almost as much as the artists on stage. Later, Joanna travels to the Kiso Valley to walk the Nakasendo Way, an ancient route that once linked Tokyo to Kyoto, a place best known for that most famous of Japanese traditions, the Geisha

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Charlotte sets out to avoid Sidney, until a serious accident forces them together and to reappraise each other. When Tom brings a new doctor to the town, he and Lady Denham clash, while Clara makes a bold move in her bid to outplay Sir Edward and Esther. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Charlotte Spencer, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky has moved in with her rich aunt Matilda, and seems to have a bright future ahead of her. However, war is brewing on the horizon, which threatens the fortunes of the scheming social climber and everyone she knows. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Attempts to scare off Robert's other mother leave him little to crow about

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fox tells the court how Vicky bribed him to change his statement, and her barrister paints a picture of Steve taking advantage and using her money

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is distraught over Steve's sentence and the fact Vicky walked free. The Duckworths return from Spain and Betty is relieved to hand the pub back to them

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tom goes to school to explain himself to the headmaster. Viv follows Donna to find out where Kelly is staying. Rachel spreads the gossip about Eric getting engaged

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers visit Dave's grave on what would have been his birthday. Frank and Chris are arrested for conspiracy to commit fraud. Eric's fiancee Dee arrives in the village

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan starts dating a posh new girlfriend, but makes the mistake of asking Rigsby for advice on how to conduct himself in polite company - a sure-fire recipe for disaster. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Osborne seemingly dies after eating a bowl of Miss Jones' home-made soup, plunging the household into mourning - but Rigsby is the one who ends up being measured for a coffin. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida's birthday celebrations give her the blues, and even Wilfred's arrival doesn't make her any happier

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is interviewed by the police about the hijacking of one of his lorries five years ago. Marlon attempts a set-up to make it look like the Dingle pigs have been stolen

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie hands Frank her resignation, and he is desperate to find Kim before she can do anything else to ruin him. Vic and Viv have a public row in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is sickened to find she and Steve are on the front page of the Gazette, and the McDonalds visit Steve in prison. Tricia starts work at Baldwin's Sportswear

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Betty tells Alec that Vicky needs a fresh start somewhere else. Vera and Jack spy on each other to catch the other dipping into the till. Anne's parents do not want to meet Andy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As campaigning for the 1970 general election gets under way in Oxford, racial tensions escalate in the city and a clash between two rival gangs leads Morse and Thursday to the door of a familiar face, where they discover the influence of a right wing organisation hoping to win an Oxford seat. Things get worse at an Indian restaurant, where a mysterious disappearance and a shocking murder put even the strongest family loyalties to the test. Crime drama, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: With their friendship in tatters, the lack of communication between Rachel and Janet is making even the simplest of tasks difficult, and it takes a call about Gill's possible abduction to reunite them as a team. The duo soon have Gill's car in their sights and identify the kidnapper, but discover they are heading for a notorious suicide location. Can the detectives get to their boss in time?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert, Angie and Ida's move north goes from bad to worse when they eventually arrive only to discover the furniture hasn't

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida finds her new home uncomfortable, until a special delivery arrives

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Police ask Sarah about the accident, and why she called 999 before the collision happened. In Roy's Rolls, Elaine introduces herself to Sally, and explains that she is Tim's mother. And Jenny sympathises with Abi over the twins' imminent departure for Australia

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally implores Tim to accept that Elaine could be telling the truth and Geoff could have fed him a pack of lies all his life. Elsewhere, Leanne is overwhelmed with grief, and when Carla lets slip that Gary's binned his threat of a rent increase, Maria's concerned

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff swears that Elaine is peddling lies, she's not Tim's Mum and Alya must have put her up to it in a bid to drive a wedge between them. Leanne urges Toyah to set the fostering wheels in motion as she deserves some happiness. And Adam seethes when Sarah fails to mention Gary's change of heart over the rent

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The body of Dr Stephen Black is found on an Oxford tour bus, and the detectives are led to sprawling Crevecoeur Hall, where Hathaway spent much of his childhood. As he investigates, he is drawn to Scarlett Mortmaigne, the glamorous daughter of the estate's owner, but when another corpse is discovered, Hathaway's refusal to believe the family is involved threatens to test his relationship with Lewis to breaking point. Guest starring Nathaniel Parker (The Inspector Lynley Mysteries), Juliet Aubrey (Primeval) and Stella Gonet (Holby City), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of prison officer Charlie Tyner, Brooks and Devlin discover he had been abusing his position and convicts in his charge. A suspect is brought to trial, but Steel and Phillips go in pursuit of a career criminal connected to Tyner and have to persuade their key witness to give evidence against a man she claims to love. Starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels along the Staffordshire and Worcestershire canal, widely regarded as one of the oldest and prettiest waterways in Britain. On his journey he marvels at ingenious 18th-century engineering, tackles the difficult skill of glassblowing and discovers a secret underground cold war bunker

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida and Angie brace themselves for a visit by Robert's 'other mother'. But they're not prepared for following events
