programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps man to create a fun bedroom for his two daughters. She creates a pinboard using a bold vintage fabric, and a garland from the felt offcuts. Plus, the girls help to personalise some jewellery boxes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi help retired couple Matthew and Myra McGuigan search for a new home in either Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, or Spain's Costa del Sol. The couple have already sold their UK house and are looking to find another property quickly

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are on the Wirral, where Simon has a plan to turn a chunky chain into a standard lamp and Henry picks up a 1963 Ford Anglia to get it back to its best

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is near Preston, where Simon O'Brien restores a spotlight he believes may be from a German U-boat while Henry Cole gets creative with an aircraft jump seat

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Krys have their first gastronomic weekend, and the pressure is on to make sure it runs smoothly. Belinda and Lee are hosting their biggest event so far with music, dancing and a slap-up meal for paying guests from the surrounding towns and villages. However, it seems they might have underestimated the wine requirements of the British contingent. Dick Strawbridge visits Angela and Steve to lend a hand with a precariously dislodged beam holding up the roof of one of their chateau turrets

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Mandy and Tony Slater to build their dream home directly opposite their old one in the Cotswolds, but the groundworks threaten to derail their modest budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly one-eyed South African porcupine Roxy is introduced to a new, younger male called Henning, in the hope that she will find companionship in her old age. Kifaru, the oldest eastern black rhino at Chester Zoo, meets younger female Malindi, with the expectation that they will produce offspring to increase the dwindling population, while the Caribbean flamingos are breeding again

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel tries to fix a cocker spaniel's leg problems by fitting two bionic back feet, before launching a series of investigations into what is causing a cat to drag its leg. Plus, a Westie puppy with a fractured knee arrives, but it cannot keep still

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge helps Lincolnshire couple Tim and Rebecca find their feet after leaving the UK to start a new life in a dilapidated 25-room chateau that has no plumbing

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Michelle Parsons, an artist and teacher who decided to seize the day and build her own private hideaway in a woodland environment in Essex after she recovered from a serious illness. Together with her architect husband David, Michelle planned to construct a sleek, black-clad, three bedroom house complete with an art studio. However, torrential rain and a potentially disastrous gas leak stalled progress on the build, and as construction gets under way, Kevin wonders whether the couple's decision to eliminate windows on two sides of the property may leave them living in a gloomy bunker

programme.title: Inside the Zoo: Down Under
programme.desc: Taronga attempts to create history by successfully exporting two platypuses to America, while keepers try to keep four elephants cool as Australia swelters in record temperatures

programme.title: The Billion Pound Hotel
programme.desc: Shaped like a giant sail and standing more than 1,000ft tall, the Burj Al Arab in Dubai cost an estimated £1billion to build, and has been dubbed the world's most luxurious hotel. Guests paying anything up to £10,000 a night expect only the highest of standards, so what does it take for the staff to satisfy these demanding customers? This programme follows the stories of a variety of visitors, from the King of Uganda to a firefighter who has saved up months of wages, and the workers who serve them

programme.title: The World's Most Extreme
programme.desc: Incredible vehicles from around the globe, featuring the biggest dump truck and the tallest telescopic crane, as well as wild off-road races in the Mexican desert

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and David O'Doherty compete against Jon Richardson and Natasia Demetriou. John Kearns joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Building the Dream

programme.title: Border Interceptors

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to discover if they can go motor racing for less than it costs to play golf. There's also a double helping of guests, with rock star Slash and Formula One driver Kimi Raikkonen taking to the track in Reasonably Priced Cars. Richard also gets behind the wheel of the BMW M5, which is powered by a twin-turbo V8 engine instead of the high-revving V10 of its predecessor

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compete in a 1950s-style race from London to Edinburgh. On the footplate of the A1 Tornado steam train, Jeremy has to feed the boiler with a constant supply of coal, while Richard braves the saddle of a Vincent Black Shadow motorbike and James drives an old Jaguar XK120 as the trio discover what Top Gear might have been like 60 years ago. Michael Schumacher drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Kryten falls head over heels in love with Camille, a female mechanoid rescued from a crashed vessel. However, the object of his desire is not all she appears to be. Starring Robert Llewellyn, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules and Chris Barrie

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew finds a machine capable of transforming the shape of any living thing by altering its genetic make-up, a discovery resulting in a desperate fight for survival. Starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew lands on a prison planet where crime is prevented by a 'Justice Field', but Rimmer finds himself convicted by his own feelings of guilt of 1,169 murders - the crew of Red Dwarf, who died after he failed to replace a drive plate properly. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles and Danny John-Jules

programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.desc: Guests Miles Jupp, Deirdre O'Kane and Phill Jupitus join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'non sequiturs' in this extended episode. programme.desc: Officers are on alert for a cartel ambush as they transfer a Sicario to the Mexican military. A random inspection of a pick-up truck reveals 32kg of narcotics

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A vast project in the icy wilderness of southern Alaska, and a landscape of confusing and surprising structures that represented a bold statement of Spain's plans for the future

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef ends his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures by visiting Bangladesh, sampling the curry on offer in the country that has played a major role in establishing the success of Indian restaurants throughout the UK

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May put three supercars through their paces in Italy. Their weapons of choice are the 691bhp Lamborghini Aventador, McLaren's hi-tech MP4-12C, and a British brute in the form of the Noble M600. The journey begins at Nardo, a high-speed testing ground so vast it can be seen from space, and includes a nerve-racking drive in Rome before reaching its conclusion with a timed challenge against the Stig at the Imola circuit

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May travel to Beijing to learn about China's car industry, which has seen great expansion in recent years - but also have a violent encounter with one of the Stig's cousins. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond immerses himself in the world of Nascar at a race in Texas, and the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG Roadster is wrestled around the test track, emitting serious amounts of noise and smoke in the process. Future host Matt LeBlanc is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: In the company of comedian Rob Delaney, Richard Ayoade packs his carry-on case and sets off to enjoy an optimal two-day mini city break in Seville, southern Spain. On arrival, the duo soon learn to dance flamenco and eat tapas and churros. They also explore Metropol Parasol, one of the boldest pieces of modern architecture in Europe and visit a film set built for the spaghetti western A Fistful of Dollars. After seeking to channel their inner Eastwood, the amigos repair to the saloon for some Fanta and a Kinder egg

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by Jo Brand for a two-day spell in the Italian city. The pair stay at the famous Hotel Danieli, formerly the lodgings of Lord Byron, Charles Dickens and Indiana Jones, and in almost no time learn to row a gondola, take in the famous sights, sample the Venetian version of tapas, and explore the world of masks. They also road-test Whaiwhai - a book used with a map and mobile that takes people on a treasure hunt around the city - only without the treasure

programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Adam Gendle travel around the country to complete a variety of outlandish stunts, beginning with an attempt to bounce a man across a lake and into a target

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: The MV Helgafell, a reinforced-hulled vessel designed to operate throughout the harsh North Atlantic

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: A profile of the Royal Clipper, officially the largest square-rigged ship in the world, which operates as a glamorous high-end cruise ship for sailing enthusiasts

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies
programme.desc: Archive performances by post-punk acts including Ian Dury and the Blockheads, the Police, Simple Minds and Madness

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Recalling a decade in British music, with archive performances from the early stages of the 'swingin' era, featuring music by by rock, pop and R'n'B artists including the Springfields, the Shadows, the Beatles and the Rolling Stones

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Staff cope with the pressure of rush hour during the summer, with Lorel Kinghorn trying to get a group of rowdy schoolchildren safely on board a train without causing major delays on the line. There is a chance to find out what happens during the routine weekly maintenance, and Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and Emma Taylor take to the air as part of their commitment to preserve some of the most historic parts of the railway

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Staff carry out their duties through the night, with Reading's duty station manager Gary Brownlie trying to stop students dodging train fares. Paul Eddolls works a typical late shift in Bristol, where sleepy passengers and drunken revellers make his job difficult, and a service disruption causes chaos for Dean Haynes at London's busy Ealing Broadway station

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Racegoers head to two major meetings at Newbury Racecourse, with the company sending staff to a usually unmanned station to help keep things running smoothly. Paddington controller Dave Turner ensures that the correct train arrives at the intended platform at the right time, and the emergency services are called into action at the Penzance depot

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: The documentary explores the intelligence of marine mammals, discovering just how smart whales and dolphins really are. Scientist Denise Herzing talks about the development of a two-way communication system between dolphins and humans in the Bahamas, while in Western Australia the creatures are filmed using a range of survival skills. In Mexico, grey whales seemingly empathise with humans, while the humpbacks of Alaska pool their talents to increase hunting success. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: The documentary explores the intelligence of marine mammals, discovering just how smart whales and dolphins really are. Scientist Denise Herzing talks about the development of a two-way communication system between dolphins and humans in the Bahamas, while in Western Australia the creatures are filmed using a range of survival skills. In Mexico, grey whales seemingly empathise with humans, while the humpbacks of Alaska pool their talents to increase hunting success. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River

programme.title: Sounds of the Seventies
programme.desc: Archive performances by post-punk acts including Ian Dury and the Blockheads, the Police, Simple Minds and Madness

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Recalling a decade in British music, with archive performances from the early stages of the 'swingin' era, featuring music by by rock, pop and R'n'B artists including the Springfields, the Shadows, the Beatles and the Rolling Stones

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Sordid speculation dogged the life of Prince Albert Victor, who was even a suspect in the Jack the Ripper case

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A two-hour special featuring a deserted Caribbean city, an American ghost town still burning and an Arctic settlement facing a terrifying future

programme.title: Monster Ships
programme.desc: The MV Helgafell, a reinforced-hulled vessel designed to operate throughout the harsh North Atlantic

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: War Factories

programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John

programme.title: History's Greatest Hoaxes
programme.desc: The forgery of Hitler's diaries in the 1980s, which fooled several respected historians and news publications, and brought to light the thriving trade in Nazi memorabilia

programme.title: History's Greatest Hoaxes
programme.desc: The story of Orson Welles' infamous radio adaptation of HG Wells' The War of the Worlds, which many Americans mistook for a news broadcast about a real alien invasion

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston goes in search of the location of the real Ark of the Covenant, believed to be held in a chapel in Ethiopia that is now in dire need of repair

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The desert town that faced a disaster of biblical proportions. Plus, scorched remains that speak of imprisonment and torture and vast coastal ruins that reveal a past once wiped from history

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of a set of Second World War sea defences built near the mouth of the River Thames to protect London. Plus, a concrete pyramid build during the Cold War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Ayr to Skye and investigates the parallel 'roads' etched in the side of Glen Roy - one of the geological mysteries of the 19th century. He also finds out how the Victorians put a weather observatory on the top of Ben Nevis, and crosses the Glenfinnan viaduct by steam train

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the final leg of his journey from Ayr to Skye, Michael Portillo discovers how the railways provided Second World War commandos with the means to travel to the isolated landscape of Lochailort, where they were able to train without attracting the attention of German spies. He also learns why langoustines have replaced herring as the best catch in the fishing port of Mallaig, and explores the history of Highland crofters

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Plymouth's Royal William Yard. Items brought in to be valued include a ceremonial wooden spoon presented to the student who got the worst exam results in the history of Cambridge University, one of the last flasks of naval rum to be produced, a book of designs by a famous landscape gardener, a ceremonial hammer and chisel and some dolls' house furniture. One visitor discovers the brooch she bought in a charity shop is much older than she thought

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present for his wife

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: The railroads ended slavery in the USA, but trains can be agents of oppression too. The British Empire built railways to rule the world, enabling it to control the empire

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of the Vajont Dam in the mountains of northern Italy, and the catastrophic flood in 1963 that caused the deaths of 2000 people

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns more about the country's railways, beginning with the Great Eastern Line, which runs along the edge of England and into central London. On his travels, the former politician discovers the grave-robbing history of Great Yarmouth and tries his hand at working a Victorian swing bridge in Reedham, Norfolk. He also takes to the air to reveal how a rail guidebook helped aviators during the Second World War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey along the Great Eastern Line, which ventures from the edge of England to the heart of London. In this leg, he examines the Victorians' fascination with the Suffolk village of Dunwich, Britain's answer to Atlantis, and discovers how nearby Felixstowe became home to the UK's biggest container port

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Documentary exploring unusual rail networks from around the world, beginning with a look at trains in cities, including an inverted monorail in Wuppertal, Germany. With contributions from Susana Esteves da Fonseca

programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion

programme.desc: An insight into the production of Ford's Mustang, which was designed to conform to the firm's objective of building machines that appealed to an international consumer base

programme.title: Incredible Engineering Blunders: Fixed
programme.desc: Former aerospace engineer Justin Cunningham and Tomo Umewaka find solutions for engineering mistakes. In this episode Justin tries to keep the Glasgow Tower turning

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: More everyday items go under the microscope. How are objects including raw pet food and replica police lanterns made?

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: A look at how everyday objects such as basketry sculptures and coffee roasters are made, and how techniques have been refined over time

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wiltshire mother and daughter Emma and Charlotte have a budget of £100,000 for a holiday home in Antequera in Spain where they hope to spend more quality time together as a family. Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops to show five suitable properties

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Chocolate shop manager Ginnette hosts the first of a week of dinner parties from in and around Sheffield, hoping to impress her guests with a menu of fun family favourites

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second host in Sheffield is emergency services worker Josh, who entertains his guests with life drawing - but a furry find during the arrival drinks divides opinion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: GP surgery manager Clare hosts the third dinner party of the week in and around Sheffield, hoping to impress her guests with a 'winter warmer' menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wellness coach Andy is the fourth host in and around Sheffield. He hopes to impress his guests with a tasty and healthy meal - and livens up the proceedings with his singing skills

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host in and around Sheffield is 37-year-old psychologist Abi, who hopes to win her guests over with a menu inspired by her travels through Asia. There's an impromptu lesson in sock puppetry, which is both a hit and miss with the group, while a burning hot chilli sauce and some supermarket-bought fish proves cause for controversy with a competitive bunch who are ready to pounce on any missteps

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Rodriguez family help Keith and Julie from Staffordshire find a villa in Alicante. Elsewhere, the Garners take part in a bowling match against their local rivals in Almeria

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: A Huddersfield couple, having married for the second time, are looking to build a new life in Spain, while a retired builder from Reading wants a holiday home in Alicante

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sharon and Steve search for the perfect property in Almería for Somerset woman Vanessa, who wants a rural property to retire to with her husband Mark. In Alicante, Tony and Martina from Oxfordshire are on the hunt for a holiday apartment for their three teenage children

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Louisa and Chris from Sussex search for their first ever home together, while Sharon and Matt road-test a new 360-degree camera that allows clients to view properties from home

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Ruth from Colchester, Essex, wants to move to Spain to give her and her son Hayden a better quality of life. As well as finding a home for themselves with a maximum budget of 225,000 euros, Ruth is after somewhere that could provide extra income too. Sharon and Steve come up with three unusual properties, including one that features a separate restaurant, sports hall, and 200-seater amphitheatre. The Rodriguez family helps retired hairdresser Janet, who brings along her cousin Sheila to help find her ideal Spanish getaway on a budget of 70,000 euros

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peter's Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tina's work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef heads to the Seascape restaurant in the village of Islip on Long Island, New York, in a bid to change the fortunes of its owner, who is nearly $1million in debt. Gordon is dismayed to discover unprotected meat and fish left next to mouldy pesto jars in the kitchen and plans to close down the eatery until radical changes are made

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Another chance to see the special Stand Up to Cancer edition of the TV review series, originally shown during the night of fundraising in October 2018, with Louis Tomlinson and Liam Payne, Danny and Dani Dyer, Dele Alli and Eric Dier, and Peter Crouch and Abbey Clancy taking to their sofas. What do they think of The Haunting of Hill House, The X Factor, University Challenge, The Yorkshire Vet, Naked Attraction and more?

programme.title: Travel Man's Greatest Trips
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade recalls some of the finest meals he has enjoyed on his trips. The memories begin with breakfast in Zurich with Frank Skinner, then a perfect macaron in the company of Mel Giedroyc in Paris. In Barcelona, Richard and Kathy Burke took a masterclass in cava and he learned the secrets of chocolate-making in Brussels with Lee Mack. Richard sampled 14 courses of molecular gastronomy with Dawn French in Athens, and in Hong Kong he and Jon Hamm went in search of street food. Along with Paul Rudd, he sampled a local delicacy in Helsinki, and in Russia some space food with Greg Davies and a Soviet-era meal with Rob Beckett

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Another selection of memorable stories filmed at King's College and St George's hospitals in south London, with this edition focusing on patients and staff who have all overcome personal setbacks and adversity. Eve, 83, has fallen down the stairs into her basement and has an open ankle fracture that needs urgent realignment to restore the blood flow to her leg, while four-year-old Maddi has fallen over and injured her neck on a train

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree host their last wedding of the year, and they welcome a fleet of vintage Citroens, whose drivers are making a pit-stop on a charity tour around France. After a sumptuous breakfast, the cars are on their way, and Dick, Angel, Arthur and Dorothy can focus on a retro vehicle of their own, the 1980s Asquith bus. With Angel's unique design, and Dick's engineering, it soon becomes the camper van the family dreamed of

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and Dr Helen Lawal challenge three pairs of friends to try out the latest popular diets, while cook Stacie Stewart demonstrates some healthy recipes. Sophie and Zoe give the Citrus Diet and the Sexy Pineapple Diet a road test, Barbara and Denise spend six weeks on the Summer Salad Diet and the Summer Sandwich Diet ahead of their holiday in Ibiza, and Theo and Ervis take on the Greek Island Longevity Diet and the South American Diet so they can lose weight in time for a pool party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee accountant Annika hosts the first dinner party in Boston, Lincolnshire, where she hopes to impress farmer's wife Carolyn, electrical engineer Adrian, carer Sylwia, and glamorous mum Katy with an evening modelled on a country fayre

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Competitions fanatic Katy hosts the second night in Boston, Lincolnshire, where she aims to impress her guests with her organisation skills and a menu featuring savoury profiteroles. However, fellow contestant Carolyn is critical of the food, and lone male host Adrian struggles to feel included when the conversation turns to hair extensions and fake eyelashes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, Adrian hopes that his spicy food and surprises will help him scoop the £1,000 prize. Although he was quiet at the table the previous night, Adrian goes on the charm offensive and seems to be impressing his guests - but he may have gone a step too far with his final revelation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Polish-born carer Sylwia hosts the fourth party of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, and hopes her European menu will charm her guests. However, the growing friction between diners Annika and Carolyn threatens to spoil the evening, and the host's chances of winning the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party night in Boston, and the hard-to-please foxhunter Carolyn is planning on using a charm offensive to win over the guests after having criticised each of them during the week. She has offended Annika, called Katy fake, found fault with Sywia's food, and reprimanded Adie, but still believes she may be able to turn things around

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sherry and her partner Phil kick-off the contest in Oxfordshire, with a traditional menu with a modern twist served in their grand dining room. On day two, competitive couple Jade and Luke rely on their menu of happy hearty food as their winning ticket. On the final night, Zahra and Conall plan to impress their guests with plates of perfectly presented food. Old smoothie Phil gives romance-shirking Conall a few lessons in love, before a few embarrassing pet names and the £1,000 prize winners are revealed

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples host dinner parties in the Spanish resort of Marbella as they competes for the cash prize of £1,000. Stephen and his Colombian boyfriend Juan plan to keep things simple in the kitchen, but two of their guests point out that the chicken is overcooked. At Glenn and Henriette's Mexican evening, the contestants get competitive when they're let loose on a pinata, while Tristan and Louise hope to inject love into their evening - until Stephen gives them frank feedback on the food

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wiltshire mother and daughter Emma and Charlotte have a budget of £100,000 for a holiday home in Antequera in Spain where they hope to spend more quality time together as a family. Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops to show five suitable properties

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shania and Farrukh are on the hunt for the perfect family property in Florida. Ben Hillman helps by lining up five options, hoping one will become their dream holiday home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Having holidayed many times in Torrox Costa, Spain, house-hunter Judith now feels the time has arrived for her to realise her dream of finding a holiday home in the region. Laura Hamilton pulls out all the stops to show five properties within her budget of £100,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: House-hunters Charlotte and Ben are looking to move their young family lock, stock and barrel to the Central American country of Costa Rica. With their £200,000 budget, Ben Hillman shows them five family homes. However, it's a huge life-changing decision, so will any of them be the right fit for the adventurous pair?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps childhood sweethearts Michelle and Fred find their dream holiday home on Gran Canaria. Michelle and Fred fell in love with the island after holidaying there over 20 years ago, and now the time is right to use their £190,000 budget to find somewhere where they can bring Michelle's mother and their three grandsons to enjoy too

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Yanni's in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter Avgoustiou, a chef so set in his ways that he hasn't allowed any changes to his menu since the Greek restaurant opened in 1984. Peter's family is concerned the business is becoming trapped in the past, and standards are slipping, but he seems determined to stick to his guns

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Prohibition Grille in Everett, Washington, where he meets belly dancer-turned-restaurateur Rishi Brown, who is making all the wrong moves with her business. Distracted from the task of improving her menu by performing for customers, she's oblivious to the disastrous dishes being served from her own kitchen

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faces the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company, but can he get through to this defiant pair?

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Tom Allen take on Sean Lock and Rosie Jones in the classic words and numbers quiz, while Sean Bean - aka Adam Riches - joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Another chance to see the special Stand Up to Cancer edition of the TV review series, originally shown during the night of fundraising in October 2018, with Louis Tomlinson and Liam Payne, Danny and Dani Dyer, Dele Alli and Eric Dier, and Peter Crouch and Abbey Clancy taking to their sofas. What do they think of The Haunting of Hill House, The X Factor, University Challenge, The Yorkshire Vet, Naked Attraction and more?

programme.title: The Talk
programme.desc: Famous parents of black and mixed race children share their personal stories of making them aware of racism, and being given similar lessons by their own parents. Tinie Tempah, Emeli Sandé, Leigh-Anne Pinnock, Lennie James, Maro Itoje and Ade Adepitan are among the contributors discussing being on both sides of this commonplace exchange, and how they dealt with subjects like being seen as different or dealing with the police

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Steph Wilson and her husband Alex build a contemporary, cantilevered farmhouse on farmlands once owned by her grandfather in Herefordshire. The couple are told their ambitious design will cost £500,000, twice as much as they can borrow, but the pair decide to take a leap of faith and move into a draughty old caravan on site as they begin working on their new home

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick tries to save a tortoiseshell cat's leg by cutting out a tumour that has developed and replacing its knee with a unique bionic implant. Staffordshire bull terrier Rosie is admitted as an emergency after her back legs suddenly collapse in the park, and Noel performs essential spinal surgery to the diseased discs in Rosie's spine and advises a course of intense physiotherapy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Cholmeley Arms in Burton-le-Coggles, Lincolnshire, where chef John Berry and his wife Lesley hold a sausage-making competition for their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Islington Mill B&B in Salford, where Joe Spencer and Louise Woodcock have an alternative hosting style as their drag alter egos

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third host Peter Morris welcomes guests to the Pebbles B&B in Seaton, Devon, but despite his breakfast award, not all of the guests are impressed by his culinary offerings

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Tarr Farm Inn on Exmoor, where Hilary Lester and Stacey Wade are proud of their five-star standards. However, Peter is on the warpath after his feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the last day of the competition and there is tension in the air, as Cholmeley Arms hosts John and Lesley want to get to the bottom of Peter's comments

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five amateur cooks from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, take turns trying to throw the perfect dinner party. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT expert and disco fanatic Joel Rowbottom throws the second of the week's parties in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with an exciting mixture of food and dance. The host faces awkward questions about his disco pants, resulting in some frank revelations, but hopes he can turn the evening's fortunes around with his 'dad dancing' competition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Donna Stephenson hopes to impress with a main course of 'bunny delight' as she hosts the third dinner party from Wakefield, West Yorkshire - but her guests are distracted by the number of animals she has

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shoeless window cleaner Jake Coxon hosts the fourth dinner party from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with beef Wellington, and performing his song God Is a Rocking Chair with his band the Sourheads

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Francesca Saynor hosts the final dinner party in Wakefield, hoping that her simple menu and back-to-school theme will be enough to impress her guests and make her the winner of the £1,000 prize

programme.desc: House-hunters Charlotte and Ben are looking to move their young family lock, stock and barrel to the Central American country of Costa Rica. programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A cousin's wedding reunites the Cranes with a Greek branch of the family - and forces Frasier to deal with an old grudge against his aunt Zora. Kelsey Grammer stars, with a guest appearance by Broadway star Patti LuPone

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Prohibition Grille in Everett, Washington, where he meets belly dancer-turned-restaurateur Rishi Brown, who is making all the wrong moves with her business. Distracted from the task of improving her menu by performing for customers, she's oblivious to the disastrous dishes being served from her own kitchen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Mal and Sue from Suffolk, who are looking to retire to Spain. She shows them five properties around Vera, hoping one will fall within their budget of £70,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps childhood sweethearts Michelle and Fred find their dream holiday home on Gran Canaria. Michelle and Fred fell in love with the island after holidaying there over 20 years ago, and now the time is right to use their £190,000 budget to find somewhere where they can bring Michelle's mother and their three grandsons to enjoy too

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help the Clayton-Fishers, who have fallen out of love with their home in Walthamstow, east London. Sarah and her wife Pepper have opposing views on what to do with their two-bedroom terrace, as Sarah thinks it is time to move on, but Pepper is digging her heels in and wants to convert the loft

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Doctor and dietician Michael Mosley puts five volunteers on a low-calorie diet to help get their health back on track after the UK's Covid-19 lockdown. In the first episode, Michael runs a series of tests to find out just how badly lockdown has affected his volunteers' health, before creating a bespoke diet plan for the 21 days. He also tests misconceptions of what a healthy BMI looks like, and explores the cutting-edge research which indicates ties between BMI and survivability from Covid-19

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Tom Allen take on Sean Lock and Rosie Jones in the classic words and numbers quiz, while Sean Bean - aka Adam Riches - joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at a busy public employment service, revealing what life is like for those living on welfare and how the dedicated team of job coaches help customers. The first edition features 61-year-old former account worker Karen, who is struggling to compete in today's job market today, while Kenny, 32, is seen by work coach Bernie to try to get him back on his feet, and ex-cleaner and grandmother Pamela Anderson is struggling to find the time to search for a job

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Cameras focus on the five per cent of the UK's families who are collectively responsible for a staggering amount of the nation's crime - 50 per cent of criminal arrests. Police are dispatched to apprehend a drunk young man outside a nightclub. Some members of his family have amassed 193 arrests and 111 convictions between them

programme.title: Race Against the Virus: The Hunt for a Vaccine
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Covid-19 spread unstoppably throughout the world, told through the eyes of key scientists on the pandemic frontline. Personal diaries recorded by scientists and intensive care doctors give an insight into the race to identify the virus and try to unlock the secrets of its virulence, revealing how scientists came to realise the seriousness of the disease. Racing to develop a vaccine, leading scientist Professor Sarah Gilbert discusses the pressures she faces and her hopes that a vaccine can be developed

programme.title: The Talk
programme.desc: Famous parents of black and mixed race children share their personal stories of making them aware of racism, and being given similar lessons by their own parents. Tinie Tempah, Emeli Sandé, Leigh-Anne Pinnock, Lennie James, Maro Itoje and Ade Adepitan are among the contributors discussing being on both sides of this commonplace exchange, and how they dealt with subjects like being seen as different or dealing with the police

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which a property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad. Jasmine Harman is on Spain's Costa Blanca to help Wendy and Lee find a traditional countryside property. The couple, their two children, their pet Doberman and their West African parrot are planning a permanent move to Spain and would like a four-bedroom detached house with plenty of outside space, all for their budget of £150,000. Can Jasmine find their perfect place?

programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Waverley Hotel in Callander, Perthshire, where owners Gordon and Jan Halladay hope to impress their guests

programme.title: My Wife and Kids
programme.desc: Michael begins taking pills guaranteed to cure baldness - but unfortunately they cause his hair to grow in unusual striped patterns. Comedy, starring Damon Wayans

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Judith worries when Jake draws an intimate picture of one of Charlie's friends as part of a school assignment, and confides all her neuroses in her reluctant brother-in-law. Meanwhile, Alan resolves to create a wholesome environment for his son. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie agrees to meet an old flame, but is stunned to learn his former girlfriend, Jill, is now a man. The shock does not end there as Evelyn turns her attentions to her son's 'new' friend. As a relationship blossoms, the rest of the family waits to see how she will handle the inevitable revelation

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Judith's sister Liz (guest star Teri Hatcher) turns up at Jake's birthday party, to the delight of Charlie, who remembers her from a previous encounter. Humiliatingly, it seems she does not recall him - and worse, is determined to seduce Alan. Meanwhile, Rose goes to a wedding in a clown costume

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Judith suggests that Jake see a therapist to help him get over his parents' divorce. Charlie and Alan are unconvinced the split is to blame for the boy's mood, while Evelyn uses the situation as an excuse to offload her own resentment. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie forms a relationship with Alan's divorce attorney, whose demands quickly become excessive even for the womanising musician - but the short-lived flirtation threatens to jeopardise his brother's chances of obtaining the settlement he wants. Comedy, guest starring Heather Locklear

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan starts sleepwalking, and Charlie feels duty-bound to cure him. Meanwhile, Jake starts seeing a therapist. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake's inappropriate remarks at a meeting of Judith's divorcee support group prompt his furious mother to tell Alan she does not want her son living with Charlie any longer. The womanising musician goes to the next meeting and struggles to convince the ladies he has a good influence on his nephew

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie makes a doctor's appointment for a vasectomy - but decides to freeze some sperm in case he ever wants children. However, the simple procedure of producing a sample seems destined to fail as his family and friends manage to thwart one attempt after another. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie hurts Alan's feelings by asking him to stay out of the house while he has friends over, so Rose steps in to cheer him up - but finds his gloom hard to dispel. Sean Penn, Elvis Costello and Harry Dean Stanton guest star

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is encouraged by Charlie to return a keepsake he stole from Judith during their divorce. In doing so, he learns she has a new boyfriend - a revelation that makes him even more depressed. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Jon Cryer

programme.desc: Documentary giving an insight into cash-strapped Britain from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments, including High Court enforcement bailiffs and repossession teams. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian O'Shaughnessy and Graham Aldred's first case sees them in a Brighton health spa as they search for assets to seize in pursuit of a £20,000 debt. Following a stand-off, they devise a way to work with the owner and her professional advisers to come up with a solution. Later, Graham reveals his softer side when he revisits a woman who defaulted on a debt payment plan

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve's first job is an eviction with a twist when they find out the landlord is the tenant's son, while their next case is also tricky and involves family members with a question mark over their immigration status who are now facing homelessness. Their final case involves a man with a history of mental illness who has reached the end of his tenancy but refuses to leave, and Steve tries to find a solution after a church minister and his mother fail to placate him

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection. Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy try to collect a debt from a technology company, but with the director nowhere to be seen, the office manager does everything she can to prove the business is no longer trading. Stewart McCracken and Elmor Victor face a heartbreaking house repossession in Merseyside, where two landlords are forced to evict a tenant recovering from a brain tumour which has cost him his job and income

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy get into a doorstep confrontation with a debtor who wants to prove her family business is bankrupt. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner get caught up in a dispute between a landlord and a tenant who refuses to move out, and Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin chase a £17,000 debt. programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

Plus, a man is almost £30,000 in debt and in danger of repossession after his tenants stopped paying the rent

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The deaths of two homeless men within a day of each other prompts Green and Cassady to investigate a possible connection between the cases. Crime drama, starring Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order

Ethan claims he has been in witness protection all this time, but now the Aufiero crime family is after him

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Motorway Patrol

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Return of the show in which Dominic Littlewood reveals the innovative methods the police and members of the public are using to catch criminals in the act. In the first edition, CCTV cameras are used in a bid to help identify someone who has been making hoax calls to the emergency services, and the presenter meets an angry daughter who has hatched a clever plan to catch the carer she believes has been stealing from her parents

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood recounts the tale of a burglar who jumped from an upstairs window after being confronted by a homeowner, but accidently left behind an item of clothing later used as evidence against him. Plus, how police employ the latest technology to catch speeding motorcyclists

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: Officers investigate reports that a large dog suspected of being a banned breed has been used by its owner to threaten door staff outside a nightclub in Bridlington, East Yorkshire, so a team arms itself with stun guns and protective gear to seize the potentially dangerous animal. Plus, neighbourhood police in Cleethorpes, North East Lincolnshire, unwittingly unearth a cannabis den while looking into a bike theft

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: Taser-trained PC Nikki Cammiss is called into action to hunt down a man in Bridlington, East Yorkshire, who is suspected of attacking someone with a knife, while in Hull, a crowd of youths causes a nuisance outside a fast-food restaurant. Presented by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: In Bridlington, East Yorkshire, police officers have their hands full dealing with a rowdy drunk who has been involved in a disturbance with staff at a local nightclub. Meanwhile, in Hull, angling gear and alcohol are discovered stashed in park bushes, and the team suspects someone is planning to return later that night for a spot of illegal fishing

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: Officers in Hull chase a drink-driver fleeing the wreckage of a van that has slammed into the lights at a pedestrian crossing. On an industrial estate in Grimsby, Lincolnchire, a youth escaping from the police falls through a factory roof and suffers a broken wrist, while two well-known tower blocks are razed to the ground to make way for the regeneration of a housing estate in Hull. Presented by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: Humberside Police suspect there is a cannabis farm in a terraced property and neighbourhood officers are preparing to pay it a surprise visit. Meanwhile, members of the UK Border Agency search local businesses for immigrants who are living or working in the country illegally

programme.title: Stop, Search, Seize
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Ireland's border force officers as they fight to stop gangs of organised smugglers from bringing drugs, money, guns, cars and even exotic animals into the country

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports as customs and immigration officers tackle drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings

programme.title: Strike It Lucky

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds.
